title: Sentinel: VC/VP Engine Integration (verify_vc, verify_vp, create_vp, DID resolver)
description: Integrate a library capable of verifying JWT-VC and JWT-VP (W3C VC Data Model v2.0, VC-JOSE-COSE) and create wrapper functions in a shared library used by all sentinel components. The engine must resolve DIDs (did:key local derivation, optional did:ethr via chain), verify credential signatures, check expiry/nbf, validate VP audience binding and nonce, and report structured error codes.
objectives: Implement verify_vc(vc_jwt, trusted_issuer_dids, now) returning VerifiedCredential or raising VCError
Implement verify_vp(vp_jwt, expected_aud, expected_nonce, expected_env, now) returning VerifiedPresentation or raising VPError
Implement create_vp(vcs, holder_did, holder_key, aud, nonce, env, exp_seconds) returning signed JWT-VP
Implement DIDResolver with did:key local derivation and optional did:ethr lookup via chain
Define structured error codes: VC_EXPIRED, VC_NBF, ISSUER_UNTRUSTED, STATUS_REVOKED, SIGNATURE_INVALID, VP_INVALID, VP_AUD_MISMATCH, VP_NONCE_MISMATCH, DID_UNRESOLVABLE, SCHEMA_MISMATCH
Write test suite with known-good and adversarial vectors covering all error paths

steps.title: Choose and install VC/VP library
steps.detail: Use PyJWT + cryptography for JWT parsing and signature verification. Install: `pip install PyJWT>=2.8 cryptography>=42`. For DID key material handling install `multiformats` or implement minimal multicodec prefix decoding manually (Ed25519: prefix 0xed01, base58btc multibase). Do NOT use DIDKit if it brings native binary dependencies that complicate cross-platform builds — verify tradeoffs first. If DIDKit is chosen, pin the wheel version and include in pyproject.toml extras.

steps.title: Create module layout
steps.detail: Create `libs/common/vc_engine/__init__.py` exporting: `verify_vc`, `verify_vp`, `create_vp`, `DIDResolver`, `VCError`, `VPError`, `VCErrorCode` enum. Sub-modules: `resolver.py` (DID resolution), `verifier.py` (VC/VP verification logic), `builder.py` (VP construction), `errors.py` (exception hierarchy).

steps.title: Implement DIDResolver
steps.detail: Class DIDResolver with method `async resolve(did: str) -> DIDDocument`. For `did:key`: parse the multibase-encoded public key bytes from the method-specific-id. Ed25519 keys use multicodec prefix 0xed01. Reconstruct the JWK or raw public key bytes. Return a DIDDocument with `verificationMethod[0].publicKeyJwk`. For `did:ethr`: forward to optional ChainClient.get_did_document(did); if chain client not available, raise DID_UNRESOLVABLE. Cache resolved docs in an LRU cache (max 512 entries, TTL 300s). Example did:key: `did:key:z6Mk...` → base58btc decode → strip multicodec prefix → 32-byte Ed25519 public key.

steps.title: Implement verify_vc
steps.detail: Signature: `async def verify_vc(vc_jwt: str, trusted_issuer_dids: set[str], resolver: DIDResolver, now: float | None = None) -> VerifiedCredential`. Steps: (1) Decode JWT header without verification to get `kid` or `iss`. (2) Resolve issuer DID via resolver. (3) Verify JWT signature using resolved public key. (4) Validate `typ` header = `vc+jwt`. (5) Extract `vc` claim body. (6) Check `exp` > now (raise VC_EXPIRED). (7) Check `nbf` <= now if present (raise VC_NBF). (8) Check `iss` is in `trusted_issuer_dids` (raise ISSUER_UNTRUSTED). (9) Check `jti` is present (unique credential ID). (10) Return VerifiedCredential dataclass with fields: issuer_did, subject_did, credential_type, claims dict, exp, nbf, jti, raw_jwt.

steps.title: Implement verify_vp
steps.detail: Signature: `async def verify_vp(vp_jwt: str, expected_aud: str, expected_nonce: str | None, expected_env: str | None, resolver: DIDResolver, now: float | None = None) -> VerifiedPresentation`. Steps: (1) Decode and verify JWT. (2) Validate `typ` = `vp+jwt`. (3) Check `aud` matches expected_aud (raise VP_AUD_MISMATCH — constant-time compare). (4) If expected_nonce provided, check `nonce` matches (raise VP_NONCE_MISMATCH). (5) Extract `vp.verifiableCredential[]` list. (6) For each VC in the list call `verify_vc` with trusted_issuer_dids from context (pass through parameter). (7) Check `env` claim matches expected_env if provided. (8) Return VerifiedPresentation with: holder_did, presentation_id, vcs: list[VerifiedCredential], exp, raw_jwt.

steps.title: Implement create_vp
steps.detail: Signature: `def create_vp(vcs: list[str], holder_did: str, holder_key: Ed25519PrivateKey, aud: str, nonce: str, env: str, exp_seconds: int = 300) -> str`. Construct JWT payload: `{iss: holder_did, aud: aud, nonce: nonce, iat: now, exp: now+exp_seconds, jti: uuid4(), vp: {"@context": [...], type: ["VerifiablePresentation"], verifiableCredential: vcs}, env: env}`. Header: `{alg: "EdDSA", typ: "vp+jwt", kid: holder_did + "#" + method_fragment}`. Sign with Ed25519PrivateKey using PyJWT `jwt.encode`. Return compact JWS string.

steps.title: Define error hierarchy
steps.detail: In `errors.py`: `class VCError(Exception): code: VCErrorCode; detail: str`. `class VPError(Exception): code: VPErrorCode; detail: str`. Enum `VCErrorCode(str, Enum)`: VC_EXPIRED, VC_NBF, ISSUER_UNTRUSTED, STATUS_REVOKED, SIGNATURE_INVALID, DID_UNRESOLVABLE, SCHEMA_MISMATCH, PARSE_ERROR. Enum `VPErrorCode(str, Enum)`: VP_INVALID, VP_AUD_MISMATCH, VP_NONCE_MISMATCH, VP_EXPIRED, VC_ERROR (wraps inner VCError). Never include raw JWT bytes in error detail — only jti or truncated hash.

steps.title: Status revocation check integration
steps.detail: verify_vc does NOT directly perform revocation check because that requires async access to RevocationManager (TASK-046). Instead, verify_vc validates structural integrity and signature only. Add an optional parameter `status_checker: Callable[[str, int], Awaitable[bool]] | None = None`. If provided and `credentialStatus` is present in VC payload, call `await status_checker(statusListId, statusListIndex)` and raise `VCError(STATUS_REVOKED)` if True. This allows RevocationManager to be injected without a circular import.

steps.title: Schema validation hook
steps.detail: Add optional `schema_validator: Callable[[dict], bool] | None = None` to verify_vc. If provided, call it with the decoded `credentialSubject` dict. If it returns False, raise `VCError(SCHEMA_MISMATCH)`. Default schema validator is None (no schema check). PolicyEngine (TASK-047) can inject a validator that checks required_scopes and credential_type.

steps.title: Unit test suite with adversarial vectors
steps.detail: File: `tests/unit/vc_engine/test_verify.py`. Test cases: (1) valid_vc_ed25519 — sign and verify round-trip; (2) expired_vc — exp set to past, expect VC_EXPIRED; (3) nbf_future — nbf in future, expect VC_NBF; (4) wrong_signature — flip one byte, expect SIGNATURE_INVALID; (5) untrusted_issuer — issuer DID not in trusted set, expect ISSUER_UNTRUSTED; (6) revoked_vc — status_checker returns True, expect STATUS_REVOKED; (7) vp_wrong_aud — aud mismatch, expect VP_AUD_MISMATCH; (8) vp_wrong_nonce — nonce mismatch, expect VP_NONCE_MISMATCH; (9) unknown_did — malformed did:key, expect DID_UNRESOLVABLE; (10) create_vp_then_verify — round-trip VP creation+verification. Use `pytest-asyncio` and `freezegun` for time control.

files.tests/unit/vc_engine/fixtures/: Pre-signed JWT fixtures for known-good vectors

domain_entities.name: VerifiedCredential
domain_entities.fields: issuer_did: str, subject_did: str, credential_type: list[str], claims: dict, exp: int, nbf: int | None, jti: str, raw_jwt: str

domain_entities.name: VerifiedPresentation
domain_entities.fields: holder_did: str, presentation_id: str, vcs: list[VerifiedCredential], exp: int, aud: str, raw_jwt: str

domain_entities.name: DIDDocument
domain_entities.fields: did: str, verification_method: list[dict], authentication: list[str]

interface_contracts.signature: async def verify_vc(vc_jwt: str, trusted_issuer_dids: set[str], resolver: DIDResolver, now: float | None = None, status_checker: Callable | None = None, schema_validator: Callable | None = None) -> VerifiedCredential
interface_contracts.raises: VCError with code from VCErrorCode enum

interface_contracts.signature: async def verify_vp(vp_jwt: str, expected_aud: str, expected_nonce: str | None, expected_env: str | None, resolver: DIDResolver, trusted_issuer_dids: set[str], now: float | None = None) -> VerifiedPresentation
interface_contracts.raises: VPError with code from VPErrorCode enum, inner VCError accessible via .cause

interface_contracts.signature: def create_vp(vcs: list[str], holder_did: str, holder_key: Ed25519PrivateKey, aud: str, nonce: str, env: str, exp_seconds: int = 300) -> str
interface_contracts.raises: ValueError on invalid inputs, never leaks key material

security_constraints: Never log raw JWT or private key material — only jti or SHA-256 hash
Constant-time comparison for aud, nonce, env fields to prevent timing oracles
LRU cache for DID resolution must not cache unresolvable DIDs (negative caching only with short TTL 30s)
All VC verification must check signature BEFORE trusting any claim (prevent claim injection)
VP holder DID must match iss claim — binding check prevents stolen VP replay from different holder
exp validation must use passed-in `now` clock, never system time directly (prevents test drift)
acceptance_criteria: verify_vc correctly rejects expired, revoked, signature-invalid, and untrusted-issuer credentials
verify_vp correctly rejects wrong aud, wrong nonce, and expired presentations
create_vp produces a JWT-VP verifiable by verify_vp in a round-trip test
DIDResolver resolves did:key without network access
No private key material appears in logs, errors, or exception messages
